title: Stop Taxpayer Funding of Hamas Act
short_title: Stop Taxpayer Funding of Hamas Act
plain_summary: This bill stops the US government from sending money to Gaza. Before any money can be sent, the President must write a promise that the money will not help Hamas or other terrorist groups. The President must also promise that any United Nations groups getting the money are not teaching ideas that are against Israel or Jewish people. The bill is meant to make sure US tax money does not accidentally help terrorists or groups that spread hate.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop Taxpayer Funding of Hamas Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Restriction on the Expenditure for Federal Funds in Gaza
sections.text:
The United States government cannot spend any money in the Gaza territory until the President makes a promise in writing to two specific committees in Congress. The President must promise that the money will not help any person or group that is a member of Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, or any other group that the Secretary of State has officially called a foreign terrorist organization. The President must also promise that the money will not help any person or group that is controlled by or influenced by these terrorist organizations.

The United States government also cannot spend money in Gaza through any United Nations group or office unless the President makes another written promise to the same committees in Congress. The President must promise that the United Nations group or office is not encouraging or teaching ideas or messages that are against Israel or against Jewish people.
